title: ClaraAnswers_AccountMemo
description: Structured account memo extracted from demo/onboarding calls. v1 = demo-derived, v2+ = onboarding-confirmed.

account_id.description: Unique identifier. Format: CLARA-{YYYY}-{NNN}. Auto-assigned by pipeline at ingestion using atomic counter in state.json. Never set manually.

version.description: v1 = demo-derived preliminary. v2 = onboarding-confirmed. v3+ = subsequent updates. No upper limit.

extraction_confidence.description: Computed by pipeline script based on scored field checklist — NOT self-reported by LLM. See confidence_score_breakdown.

confidence_score_breakdown.description: Programmatic scoring of the 8 major fields. Pipeline computes this; LLM does not. Each field is 1 point if non-null and non-empty. high=7-8, medium=4-6, low=0-3.

industry.description: e.g. fire_protection, sprinkler, alarm, HVAC, electrical, facility_maintenance

office_address.description: Primary office location. For multi-location clients, see additional_locations.

additional_locations.description: Stub for multi-location clients. Populate if client mentions multiple branches. Each entry has the same shape as office_address plus an optional routing_override note.

routing_override.description: Any routing rule specific to this location.

business_hours.description: When the office is staffed and calls are handled live.

timezone.description: IANA timezone string. e.g. America/Chicago. REQUIRED for agent to function correctly — flag in questions_or_unknowns if missing. Must match IANA format (Region/City). Display names like 'Eastern Time' or abbreviations like 'ET' are NOT valid — the pipeline must reject them. Validated by pattern constraint.

schedule.description: Each entry covers one or more consecutive days.

open.description: 24h format HH:MM. null = closed all day.

close.description: 24h format HH:MM. null = closed all day.

holidays_observed.description: Free text note. e.g. 'Federal holidays, office closed'. NOTE: Agent cannot programmatically reason about holidays from free text. Flag as known limitation. Phase 3+ improvement: structured holiday list.

services_supported.description: What service lines this client handles. Extracted from call. Include only explicitly mentioned services.

emergency_definition.description: What qualifies as an emergency for this client.

triggers.description: Explicit phrases or conditions that classify a call as emergency.

non_triggers.description: Things explicitly NOT classified as emergency.

ambiguous_cases.description: Edge cases where classification is unclear. Flag for human review.

clarifying_question.description: The exact question Clara should ask to determine emergency status.
clarifying_question.example: Is this an active emergency, like a sprinkler leak or alarm sounding right now?

emergency_routing.description: How to route confirmed emergency calls. NOTE: primary_contact has been removed — use escalation_chain[0] as the primary contact. This eliminates the previous dual-update problem.

escalation_chain.description: Ordered list. order=1 is the primary contact. Try each in sequence if previous fails. Pipeline and agent spec generator must always treat order=1 as primary.

transfer_timeout_seconds.description: Seconds before declaring transfer failed and moving to next in chain. Must be confirmed on onboarding — do not default silently.

transfer_fail_action.description: What Clara does if ALL escalation chain entries fail.
transfer_fail_action.example: Apologize, collect name/number/address, assure dispatch will call back within [callback_assurance_window].

callback_assurance_window.description: The specific callback timeframe Clara promises when all transfers fail. e.g. '15 minutes', '30 minutes'. MUST be confirmed on onboarding call — never default to 15 minutes. Flag in questions_or_unknowns if not stated.

transfer_fail_notification_action.description: What system action to trigger when all transfers fail. e.g. 'send SMS to dispatch', 'create urgent ServiceTrade ticket', 'page on-call manager'. Maps to {{TRANSFER_FAIL_NOTIFICATION_ACTION}} in agent template.

data_to_collect_before_transfer.description: What to capture from caller before attempting transfer.

non_emergency_routing.description: How to handle non-emergency calls (both during and after hours).

during_hours_action.description: Structured routing target for non-emergency callers during business hours. Changed from free-text to object so the agent has an actionable phone number.

action_type.description: What type of action to perform.

phone.description: Phone number to transfer to, if action_type is transfer_to_number or transfer_to_main_line.

instructions.description: Any additional handling notes. e.g. 'Ask for the scheduling department'.

after_hours_action.description: What happens to non-emergency callers after hours.
after_hours_action.example: Collect name, number, and service type. Confirm callback during next business day.

servicetrade_ticket_creation.description: Whether to create a ticket in ServiceTrade for non-emergency after-hours calls.

integration_constraints.description: Explicit rules about what Clara must or must NOT do with integrations.

rule.example: Never create sprinkler jobs. Only create inspection jobs.

call_transfer_protocol.description: General transfer mechanics, applicable to all call types.

warm_transfer.description: Does Clara announce the caller before connecting?

transfer_announcement.description: What Clara says to the caller before initiating transfer.
transfer_announcement.example: Please hold while I connect you with our on-call technician.

voice_and_persona.description: How Clara should present herself for this client.

tone_label.description: Internal human-readable tone descriptor. Does NOT map directly to a Retell parameter. The retell_config.voice_id in the agent spec is what controls the actual voice. This field informs prompt style only.

flows.description: High-level flow summaries for context. Detailed flows live in the agent spec.

office_hours_summary.description: 1-3 sentence summary of how calls are handled during business hours.

after_hours_summary.description: 1-3 sentence summary of how calls are handled after hours.

questions_or_unknowns.description: REQUIRED. Any field that could not be reliably extracted. Must be present even if empty []. Never silently omit. Pipeline script will reject memos where this key is absent.

field.description: Dot-path of the missing field. e.g. business_hours.timezone

suggested_question.description: Question to ask client to resolve this.

notes.description: Free-text notes from extraction. Keep under 200 chars. Flag unusual patterns.

changelog.description: REQUIRED. Append-only log of changes across versions. Never overwrite entries. Must be present even for v1 (initial entry). Pipeline script will reject memos where this key is absent.
